section1.title: What is a web application?
section1.p1: A web application is a full-fledged software product that runs in the browser. Unlike a regular website, it lets users interact with data: create, edit, manage, and analyze.
section1.p2: Examples of web applications: CRM systems, online services, dashboards, SaaS platforms, marketplaces, analytics tools, and project management systems.
section1.p3: A web application is your product that delivers value to customers or optimizes internal processes.

section2.title: When do you need a web application?
section2.lead: A web application is right for you if:
items: You want to launch a SaaS product or online service
You need a dashboard or portal for customers or employees
Complex business logic is required that a regular website can't handle
You're planning a marketplace, booking platform, or exchange
You want to automate processes and scale your business
section2.footer: If your idea goes beyond a brochure website — you need a web application.

section3.title: What do you get?
items: A complete web application designed around your business model
Authentication and role system (users, admins, moderators)
Integration with payment systems, APIs, and third-party services
Responsive interface that works on any device
Scalable architecture ready for growing traffic

section4.title: Development stages
items: Analysis and design — we study the problem, define features, design architecture and user flows.
Interface design — we create prototypes and UI focused on usability and conversion.
Development — we build frontend and backend, connect databases, APIs, and integrations.
Testing and launch — QA testing, load testing, and production deployment.
Support — monitoring, iteration, and scaling as you grow.

section5.title: Technology
section5.p1: We use a modern stack: Vue / Nuxt, React / Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes. The choice depends on project requirements.
section5.p2: For SaaS products we apply multi-tenant architecture, task queues, caching, and CDN for maximum performance.

section6.title: Security and reliability
section6.p1: We protect data at every level: encryption, JWT authentication, RBAC, validation, protection against injections and CSRF.
section6.p2: We set up automated backups, monitoring, and alerts for uninterrupted operation.

section7.title: Support and evolution
section7.p1: After launch we continue to support the project: fix bugs, add new features, and optimize performance.
section7.p2: You can scale the application, connect new modules and integrations without rewriting code.

webappFaq.q: How is a web app different from a website?
webappFaq.a: A website is an informational resource. A web application is a tool where users work with data: create, edit, manage. Examples: CRM, dashboard, SaaS service.
An MVP can launch in 6–10 weeks. A full application takes 3 to 6 months depending on complexity.

webappFaq.q: What is SaaS and is it right for me?
webappFaq.a: SaaS (Software as a Service) is a model where users pay for access to your application via subscription. If your product solves a recurring problem for many clients — SaaS is a great fit.

webappFaq.q: Can I start with an MVP?
webappFaq.a: Yes. We recommend starting with a minimum viable product to validate your hypothesis and gather user feedback.
Any: payment systems (Stripe, PayPal), email services, SMS, messengers, CRM, analytics, cloud storage, and third-party APIs.
Data encryption, JWT authentication, role-based access control, XSS/CSRF protection, logging, monitoring, and backups.

webappFaq.q: Will the app work on mobile?
webappFaq.a: Yes. We build responsive interfaces and can create a PWA (Progressive Web App) that works like a mobile app if needed.
Absolutely. The architecture is designed for growth from day one: horizontal scaling, microservices, caching.

webappFaq.q: How much does a web application cost?
webappFaq.a: Cost depends on features, complexity, and integrations. Get a preliminary estimate using our calculator.

custom.label: Custom development
pros: Fully tailored to your business logic
Full control over code and data
